name: Flown-away Birds

ingredients.item: calf’s liver, sliced about 2 cm thick
veal nut (noix, or topside), sliced about 2 cm thick

method: Serves 6 300 g calf’s liver, sliced about 2 cm thick 300 g veal nut (noix, or topside), sliced about 2 cm thick 150 g pancetta, sliced 5 mm thick unbleached plain flour, for dusting fresh sage leaves 25 g unsalted butter 25 ml good-quality virgin olive oil 2–3 cloves garlic, chopped 300 ml dry white wine salt and ground black pepper chicken or veal stock for moisture, if needed
Cut the liver, veal and pancetta into square cubes. Dust the liver and veal with flour. Then, alternating, thread the liver, sage, veal and pancetta on skewers.

Add the garlic to the pan with a few more sage leaves. Let the garlic become golden (do not burn it) then add the wine and scrape up all the bits in the bottom of the pan. When it bubbles add the skewers, salt and pepper and let them cook, covered and on low heat, for about 20 minutes.
